objective: Recover FNV parity by replaying bounded, retail-proven subsystems onto the last known-good OpenMW/OpenMW-VR visual baseline without importing the current branch's renderer regressions.

policy.migration: Re-derive or selectively transplant the smallest coherent implementation. Never merge or cherry-pick the source branch wholesale.
policy.commit: One proven slice per commit. A slice may not contain unrelated source, profile, telemetry, or generated output.
policy.closure: A slice closes only after focused tests, a hidden natural-play run, a retail differential where available, save/load coverage, and all earlier gates remain green.
policy.rollback: A failed slice is repaired or removed on the recovery branch before any later slice starts.
policy.percentage: Only closed slice gates and accepted corpus cases earn parity credit. Transplanted code alone earns zero.
policy.renderer: Static-world rendering and the Mads VR shell remain byte-for-byte at baseline unless a separately authorized renderer recovery slice identifies a baseline defect with paired evidence.

frozenBoundaries.reason: These are the known direct routes to the reflection, transparency, disappearing-world, HUD masking, pointer, menu-shader, LOD, and compositor regressions reported in live play.

exit: Clean flat and VR binaries build from the pinned commit.
The exact runtime profile, plugins, assets, and hashes are captured.

Goodsprings exterior, Doc Mitchell's house, Prospector Saloon, Strip exterior, and representative night/weather transitions render without disappearing geometry, reflection bleed, or exposure failure.
Mads VR hands, grip pointer, raycast, hover, click, close mappings, and menu shaders work unchanged.
A repeated cold-start, door-return, and save-load matrix produces the same world and actor population.
Winning FNV NPC, creature, package, idle, furniture, dialogue, quest, inventory, weapon, sound, region, weather, and mutable-reference records parse with exhaustive focused tests.
No runtime, renderer, VR, shader, or ESM3 fallback behavior changes.

Every Goodsprings NPC and creature loads on every cold start, door transition, and save/load with retail winning FormID, enable state, cell, transform, inventory, faction, actor values, and ground contact.
Trudy, Easy Pete, Doc Mitchell, saloon settlers, animals, robots, and a Deathclaw census have zero missing, duplicated, floating, or tipped instances.
The baseline world-render and VR-shell gates remain pixel- and state-clean.
Humanoids and every representative creature match retail skeleton variant, bind/root basis, idle group, blend state, hair/head attachment, weapon attachment, and vertex bounds.
All loaded Deathclaws remain upright across idle selection, interruption, combat, unload, and save/load.
No file in the frozen renderer boundary changes.

A full retail/OpenMW Goodsprings day matches package selection, routes, continuous locomotion, furniture reservation/entry/loop/exit, chore props, animal wander, robot turns, bird perches, interruption, and persistence.
There is no warping, motion-in-place, empty-hand chore, chair drift, infinite spin, or per-actor patch.
Every Goodsprings speaker/topic matches retail conditions, prompt identity, response, voice, result, next topics, and termination.
Authored INFO emotion, LIP phonemes, TRI expression weights, eyes, head, and body attention play and restore package state correctly.
No right-side option mismatch, rigid face, missing look-at, or generic invented expression remains.
The natural new-game and Goodsprings quest spine matches retail stages, objectives, variables, conditions, package overrides, result scripts, rewards, alternate branches, and save/load.
Unknown or substituted GECK commands fail closed into the corpus ledger.

Every Pip-Boy pane, inventory action, equipment mutation, ammo stack, sort/filter, barter pane, caps transfer, quest item, save/load, flat input, and unchanged Mads VR pointer path passes retail permutations.
Melee, hitscan, projectile, ammo, armor, limb damage, pursuit, attack selection, hit reaction, stagger, death, loot, XP, and save/load pass for representative NPCs and creatures.

Every Goodsprings door, vault/Strip transition, container, radio, workbench, terminal, lock, key, recipe, and scripted activator matches retail activation, condition, UI, mutation, destination, and persistence.
FNV ambience, footsteps, weapons, impacts, equipment, UI, creatures, activators, radio, music, dialogue, attenuation, and transitions resolve exact authored records/assets.
Runtime and source invariants prove zero Morrowind/ESM3 sound fallback.
All 187 capability gates and generated official-content denominators have accepted retail differential evidence.

Every base-game route, 640 quests, four story DLCs, companions, minigames, endings, clean install, performance, persistence, and crash-free soak reaches 100% with no unknown, fallback, or uncovered row.
